meta_title: For Founders: design with AI to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Design with AI to Solve No Portfolio (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle by building high-fidelity SaaS prototypes using AI. A beginner-friendly guide leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth; you don't need years of agency work to prove value. By leveraging AI-native design tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can architect, prototype, and deploy high-fidelity SaaS experiences in hours, turning your vision into a tangible asset that sells itself to stakeholders.
content.intro:
The cold reality of being a founder without a track record is the silent rejection of your ideas. You pitch, they ask for a portfolio, and you have nothing but a vision. This gap between 'idea' and 'execution' is where most startups die before they even launch.

But the rules have changed. You no longer need to be a designer or a senior dev to build a world-class interface. With the convergence of Antigravity and Claude Code, you can translate abstract concepts into market-ready SaaS prototypes. It’s time to stop talking about your product and start showing it.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Value Proposition
steps.description: Before designing, codify the core problem and solution. This sets the intent for the AI to generate high-conversion components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-converting landing page structure for a SaaS product that solves [Insert Problem]. Focus on a hero section, social proof, and a clear call to action.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a structured README.md defining the MVP scope and core features.'

steps.title: Generate UI/UX Components
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate clean, accessible code blocks. Focus on modularity to ensure the prototype is scalable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern, responsive navigation bar and hero section using Tailwind CSS. Use a professional SaaS color palette (indigo/slate).
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a components folder and inject the generated Tailwind UI code into index.tsx.'

steps.title: Implement Data-Driven Layouts
steps.description: A portfolio piece needs to look functional. Add a data dashboard or feature grid to demonstrate technical depth.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive 3-column feature grid with hover effects and icons from Lucide-React. Ensure the layout is mobile-first.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add a dashboard layout component with mock data and integration with Lucide-React icons.'

steps.title: Wireframe the User Journey
steps.description: Connect your components into a seamless flow. This proves you understand the user experience, not just the code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a multi-step onboarding flow modal component that collects user email and preferences.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create an onboarding workflow component with state management to toggle between steps.'

steps.title: Deploy the Portfolio Prototype
steps.description: Get your work live. A prototype is only a portfolio piece if it’s accessible via a public URL.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide the necessary configuration for a Vercel deployment of a React/Next.js application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure the project for Vercel deployment and verify all build dependencies.'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'perceived performance' by adding skeleton loaders to all AI-generated components; it makes the app feel enterprise-grade.
Always ask Claude Code to write unit tests for your UI components to prove you understand software engineering fundamentals, not just visual design.
Use Antigravity to generate 'Mobile-First' CSS by default; 80% of your initial stakeholders will view your portfolio on their phones.
Include a 'hidden' feature in your prototype that demonstrates complex logic, like a data filter or an API integration, to stand out from basic wrappers.
Document your AI-assisted build process in a blog post; showing how you built the product is often more valuable than the product itself.
content.next_steps: Once your prototype is live, reach out to three potential customers or investors. Don't ask for feedback—ask for a critique of the specific business problem you are solving.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the 'no portfolio' barrier dictate your success. With these tools, you are no longer just an idea-person; you are an execution-focused founder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Use Antigravity to generate clean, accessible code blocks. Focus on modularity to ensure the prototype is scalable.
A portfolio piece needs to look functional. Add a data dashboard or feature grid to demonstrate technical depth.

Get your work live. A prototype is only a portfolio piece if it’s accessible via a public URL.
